events.description: Chief of Branch of Realty H. M. Critchfield issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee, enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 office letter
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to Superintendents
Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955; committees appointed and reports developed
Committee appointed at Land Operations Conference on June 14, 1955; made preliminary report to conference on June 14
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 published amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of fees for submarginal lands
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to Superintendents
Billings Area Office memorandum transmitting committee reports from Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area at Sioux City, Iowa, June 13-15, 1955
Transmittal from Billings Area Office Branch of Realty to Superintendent, Northern Cheyenne, attaching copy of office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy
Office memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16 and subsequent policy statements
Central Office memorandum regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands and amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
Billings Area Office memorandum to Superintendents quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955 regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory or survey made of reservations by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock, transferred to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Statement on Bureau fee patent policy prepared and distributed to Area Directors in response to numerous telegrams, letters, and postal cards objecting to or inquiring about fee patent policy

financial_transactions.payee: Special Deposit (prior to August 10, 1955); Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 (after August 10, 1955)
financial_transactions.description: Fees collected for grazing privileges on submarginal lands; previously held in special deposit, now to be deposited to Treasury
Accrued construction, operation and maintenance charges required under 25 CFR 151 and Act of March 7, 1928; delinquent O&M charges must be deducted from land sale proceeds per bid form 5-114

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee

relationships.subject: Billings Area Office
relationships.context: Area office issuing directives and memoranda to superintendents under its jurisdiction

relationships.subject: Commissioner of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: Commissioner issued memorandum on fee patent policy to all Area Directors except Juneau

relationships.subject: Indian women married to white men
relationships.context: Committee recommended declaring Indian women married to white men ineligible for grazing allocations as non-Indian husbands were actual recipients of subsidy

relationships.subject: Rex Carey
relationships.context: Previously served as Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock before transfer to Pine Ridge

fee_patents.allottee_name: non-Indian who has inherited a partial interest

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in all patent requests so they can be handled expeditiously
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum distributed; Billings Area Office forwarded to Superintendents on January 6, 1955

correspondence.subject: Rights of way which are involved in the issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Obtain necessary information relating to rights of way and include such information in transmittals with applications for patents in fee or when Indian lands advertised for public sale are traversed by approved rights of way
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued; information to be obtained from agency files or from persons/organizations to whom rights of way were granted

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in memorandum of May 16 and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Policy statement distributed; temporary manual release to be issued incorporating substance of memorandum and statement

correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Any funds now held in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 before end of calendar year; hereafter revenue from submarginal lands to be deposited to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Review attached committee reports from Land Operations Conference held at Sioux City, Iowa, June 13-15, 1955 and provide comments on conclusions reached by committees
correspondence.outcome: Reports distributed with request for comment

correspondence.sender: Billings Area Office, Branch of Realty
correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy
correspondence.action_requested: For information - attached copy of office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16 and subsequent policy statements transmitted June 3 and October 4, 1955

correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds which are subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Each Superintendent to establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment collection requirements before land sale transactions are finally completed
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued following audit findings of failures to obtain payment for accrued charges

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted; created first lien against irrigable lands on all Indian irrigation projects where construction, operation and maintenance costs remain unpaid; directs lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands
Legislation pending to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; not enacted as of August 1955; special deposits discontinued pending such legislation
